originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__a_1885' during its initialization is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'a' during its initialization is never read
presenceCondition:
(or (and __STRICT_ANSI__ (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF))
    (and (and __STRICT_ANSI__ ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME))
    (and (and __STRICT_ANSI__ ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME_REGEXP))
    (and (not __STRICT_ANSI__) (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF))
    (and (and (not __STRICT_ANSI__) ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME))
    (and (and (not __STRICT_ANSI__) ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME_REGEXP)))
model:
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME_REGEXP () Bool
  false)
reasoning: a is assigned at L14 (char *a = val) and only used at L21 (s = a) inside the RENAME_REGEXP block. When CONF=T AND RENAME=T AND !REGEXP, a is a genuine dead store. No // ERROR, so this is a new bug. The PC = !(CONF AND RENAME AND REGEXP) spans many configs where L14 is not even reached in real compilation (CONF=F or RENAME=F), which are desugaring artifacts. PC is over-constrained.
